item.text: I’m going to inspect the workspace manifests directly to identify crates that *declare* `orbit-knowledge` in their own `Cargo.toml` dependency sections, then I’ll cross-check with the Orbit graph dependency view so every reported crate is verified.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps | jq -r '.packages[] | . as "'$pkg | [ .dependencies[] | select(.name=="orbit-knowledge") | (.kind // "normal") ] as $kinds | select(($kinds|length)>0) | "'"\\(.name)\\t\\(.manifest_path)\\t\\("'$kinds|join(","))"'"'"

/bin/zsh -lc "cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps | jq -r '.packages[] | . as "'$pkg | [ .dependencies[] | select(.name=="orbit-knowledge") | (.kind // "normal") ] as $kinds | select(($kinds|length)>0) | "'"\\(.name)\\t\\(.manifest_path)\\t\\("'$kinds|join(","))"'"'"

item.text: I found two strong candidates from both `cargo metadata` and manifest grep: `orbit-tools` and `orbit-cli`. I’m doing one more sweep for any alternate TOML dependency syntax so we don’t miss dev/build forms.
